behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure is at the CLI↔git boundary (rename detection from real repo state), not just local counting logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real git rename heuristics, subprocess output, and filesystem/index state can differ from fixtures; mocked status lines can pass while real `git diff --name-status` behavior fails. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: feed parser `R100 old new`, assert rename=1/add=0/del=0; catches parser bug only. System candidate: init repo, commit file, rename with `git mv`, run CLI, assert renamed bucket only; catches real handoff/flag issues. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text requires real repository change categorization. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior contains no AI/ML step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report shows exactly one renamed file and zero added/deleted for that change.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: crash risk appears when real git emits binary diff metadata and the CLI processes actual subprocess/stdout/stderr encoding paths. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: fixture text misses real binary handling edge cases (NUL bytes, 'Binary files differ' output, exit-code nuances). CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: pass canned binary-diff string to parser, assert count increments; catches parser branch bugs only. System candidate: add real binary file in repo, run CLI subprocess, assert non-crash and binary-included count; catches real decode/process boundary failures. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior explicitly concerns binary diffs from repository analysis. Rejected Agentic/Workflow due no model-dependent correctness.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp git repo, commit or modify a real binary file, execute the CLI process, and assert exit success plus expected binary-related count in the structured stdout report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: unborn-branch behavior is produced by real git commands/exit codes (`HEAD` missing), which is a subprocess interaction boundary. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked repo objects often skip real fatal messages and return-code handling, hiding failure. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: fake repo API returns 'no commits', assert empty report; catches branch logic only. System candidate: `git init` with zero commits, run CLI subprocess, assert empty structured output and zero error; catches real command-failure mapping bugs. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is about running on an actual repository state with no commits. Rejected Agentic/Workflow since no AI decision chain exists.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real empty git repository (no commits), invoke the CLI, and assert it exits successfully with an empty structured report instead of an exception/error output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: correctness depends on end-to-end interpretation of real git history/ranges, not isolated counting code. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: synthetic inputs can hide mistakes in revision selection, command flags, and subprocess parsing that only appear with actual commit graph/history. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: provide prebuilt change events to aggregator, assert totals; catches arithmetic bugs only. System candidate: construct repo history with known add/delete/rename/modify events, run CLI, assert exact counts match known history; catches real history-query and wiring failures. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly anchors to 'actual change history of the repository'. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no LLM/model output involved.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temporary git repo with a scripted sequence of commits covering add/delete/rename/modify, run the CLI, and assert report counts exactly match the known event totals from that history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is about parity between real index and working-tree states, a git/OS state boundary across subprocess calls. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: mocked status abstractions may not reproduce true staged vs unstaged semantics and command differences (`--cached` vs working tree). CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: simulate two change lists (staged/unstaged) and compare formatter output; catches formatting drift only. System candidate: in real repo create same logical change once staged and once unstaged, run CLI in both modes, assert identical structured output; catches index/worktree query mismatches. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires staged-vs-unstaged equivalence on repository state. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use a temp git repo to create identical logical changes in staged and unstaged forms, run CLI for each case, and assert the full structured stdout report is byte-equivalent (or structurally equivalent) across both.
